entities.name: PIGEON PEA
entities.description: A legume crop used to increase dietary protein and iron intake in school-aged children

entities.name: PROTEIN ENERGY MALNUTRITION
entities.description: A public health concern characterized by a lack of protein and energy, leading to growth retardation and developmental issues

entities.name: IRON DEFICIENCY
entities.description: A condition resulting from insufficient iron in the body, affecting cognitive development and health

entities.name: ANEMIA
entities.description: A condition resulting from nutrient deficiencies, specifically iron

entities.name: TANZANIA
entities.description: The country where the study on pigeon pea consumption was conducted

entities.name: CENTRAL TANZANIA
entities.description: The specific region of Tanzania where the study took place

entities.name: DODOMA DISTRICT
entities.description: The district in Central Tanzania where the study was conducted

entities.name: KONGWA DISTRICT
entities.description: The district in the Dodoma region where data were collected from caregivers

entities.name: CROSS-SECTIONAL STUDY
entities.description: The research design used to collect data at a single point in time

entities.name: QUESTIONNAIRE
entities.description: The tool used for data collection based on behavioral theories

entities.name: THEORY OF PLANNED BEHAVIOR
entities.description: A psychological theory used to design the study questionnaire to understand behavior

entities.name: HEALTH BELIEF MODEL
entities.description: A psychological model used to design the study questionnaire to understand health-related behaviors

entities.name: CORRELATIONS
entities.description: A statistical method used to assess associations between constructs

entities.name: MULTIPLE REGRESSIONS
entities.description: A statistical method used to identify predictive constructs

entities.name: MANN-WHITNEY U TESTS
entities.description: A statistical test used for score comparisons

entities.name: HEALTH VALUE
entities.description: The importance a caregiver places on preventing nutrient deficiencies in their child

entities.name: HEALTH BEHAVIOR IDENTITY
entities.description: A construct related to the identity of practicing health-promoting behaviors

entities.name: CUES TO ACTION
entities.description: Triggers that prompt a person to take action toward a health behavior

entities.name: CONTROL BELIEF
entities.description: Beliefs regarding the ease or difficulty of performing a specific behavior

entities.name: INTENTION
entities.description: The motivation or plan to consume pigeon pea

entities.name: BEHAVIOR
entities.description: The actual act of consuming pigeon pea

entities.name: PERCEIVED BARRIERS
entities.description: Factors that limit the intention to consume pigeon pea

entities.name: PEST INFESTATION
entities.description: A biological issue during storage that acts as a barrier to crop consumption

entities.name: POST-HARVEST MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Specialized practices to handle crops after harvest to reduce losses and pests

entities.name: CROP MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Agricultural practices used to ensure the productivity and supply of crops

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of crop yield and availability
